title: Delegation Credential (DC)
description: Signed document authorizing one party to act on behalf of another, with scope and time constraints. See SPEC.md §4.4. The attenuation rule (scope MUST be subset of parent) and root_operator invariant are enforced at verification time, not by this schema.

axis_version.description: Protocol version the credential's data model conforms to (e.g. "0.2", "0.3").

issued_by.description: The delegator. Either an AXIS operator ID or an AXIS agent ID. If an agent ID, parent_credential_id is REQUIRED.

issued_to.description: The delegate. An AXIS agent ID, a foreign DID (did:key, did:web, did:ethr, did:peer, etc.) per §4.4 cross-system delegation, or the v0.3 ephemeral sub-agent form axis:{operator}:{agent}:sub:{task-id} per §4.4.2.

issued_to_public_key.description: v0.3 §4.4.2: base64url Ed25519 public key of an ephemeral delegate. REQUIRED when issued_to is the ephemeral form; FORBIDDEN otherwise (enforced at verification time, not by this schema).

task_spec_hash.description: v0.3 §4.4.2: OPTIONAL SHA-256 hex of a canonical serialization of the task spec an ephemeral DC is bound to.

root_operator.description: Root operator at the top of the delegation chain. MUST be byte-for-byte identical across all credentials in a chain (root-operator invariant). Verifiers enforce this.

parent_credential_id.description: Credential that authorized the delegator. REQUIRED when issued_by is an agent (not the root operator).

scope.description: Permitted actions. MUST be equal to or a subset of the parent credential's scope (attenuation rule). Scope strings follow the v0.2 grammar (colon-separated [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ segments; single-segment wildcard *) and the v0.3 two-layer vocabulary (SPEC.md §4.4.1): standard scopes or x-<vendor>: custom scopes.

constraints.description: Additional limits. Semantics are out-of-band. Known fields below; others permitted.

max_sub_delegation_depth.description: Maximum additional sub-delegation levels permitted. 0 forbids sub-delegation.

created.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) when the credential was issued.

expires.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) after which the credential is no longer valid.

revocable.description: Whether the credential can be revoked before expiry. Defaults to true.

revocation_url.description: Where to check revocation status for this specific credential.

proof.description: Cryptographic proof per W3C Data Integrity. proofValue signs the RFC 8785 JCS canonicalization of the DC minus the proof field (SPEC.md §4.4 DC proof canonicalization).

proofType.description: Canonicalization regime selector (SPEC.md §4.4, §6.1). RECOMMENDED. "jcs-eddsa-2026" selects RFC 8785 JCS; absence means verifiers try JCS first then the deprecated legacy form; unrecognized values MUST be rejected.

created.description: OPTIONAL proof timestamp. The reference SDK does not emit it; the DC's own created field is the normative issuance time.

verificationMethod.description: Identifier of the signing key: the DID form (did:axis:prime:widget-corp:editor#key-1) or the AXIS ID form (axis:widget-corp:editor#key-1; the form the reference SDK emits).
